title: Physics-informed neural networks: A deep learning framework for solving forward and inverse problems involving nonlinear partial differential equations
summary: Foundational PINNs paper introducing neural networks trained to solve PDE-constrained forward and inverse problems. Two frameworks: continuous time models (automatic differentiation for PDE residuals) and discrete time models (implicit Runge-Kutta). Demonstrated on Navier-Stokes, Schrodinger, reaction-diffusion, and KdV equations.
relevance: Direct foundation for physics-informed loss terms in any surrogate model. The PDE residual approach is the canonical method for embedding physical laws into neural network training. All subsequent PINN-based structural engineering works build on this.

title: Physics-informed machine learning
summary: Comprehensive perspective/review on the emerging field of physics-informed machine learning. Covers PINNs, embedding physical laws (PDEs, conservation laws, symmetries) into neural network training, and applications to forward/inverse problems across science and engineering. Includes discussion of limitations and future directions including uncertainty quantification, multi-fidelity learning, and interpretability.
relevance: Essential high-level reference for the field. Provides taxonomy of physics integration strategies (observational bias, learning bias, inductive bias) that can guide the design of physics-informed objectives for structural surrogate modeling. Discusses multiscale and multiphysics extensions relevant to multi-scale PI-HGNN.

title: Physics-Informed Machine Learning: A Survey on Problems, Methods and Applications
summary: Systematic review from three perspectives: (1) ML tasks (regression, generation, PDE solving), (2) representation of physical prior (PDE, symmetry, constitutive law), (3) methods for incorporating physical prior (loss function, architecture, data augmentation). Discusses open problems including encoding physical priors into architectures and domain-specific applications.
relevance: Useful for categorizing different approaches to incorporating physics into deep learning. The taxonomy of physical prior representation helps structure the design choices for physics-informed regularization in structural surrogate modeling.

title: When Physics Meets Machine Learning: A Survey of Physics-Informed Machine Learning
summary: Surveys PIML from three aspects: motivations, types of physics knowledge, and methods of integration. Covers physics knowledge forms (PDEs, ODEs, algebraic constraints, symmetries) and integration methods (loss function penalties, architecture constraints, data augmentation). More than 236 citations.
relevance: Provides structured framework for categorizing different physics knowledge types relevant to structural engineering (equilibrium, constitutive laws, boundary conditions). Helpful for deciding which physics constraints are feasible given the available data schema.

title: Scientific Machine Learning Through Physics-Informed Neural Networks: Where We Are and What's Next
bibtex:
@article{cuomo2022scientific,
  title   = {Scientific machine learning through physics--informed neural networks: Where we are and what's next},
  author  = {Cuomo, Salvatore and Di Cola, Vincenzo Schiano and Giampaolo, Fabio and Rozza, Gianluigi and Raissi, Maziar and Piccialli, Francesco},
  journal = {Journal of Scientific Computing},
  volume  = {92},
  number  = {3},
  pages   = {88},
  year    = {2022},
  publisher = {Springer},
  doi     = {10.1007/s10915-022-01939-z}
}
summary: Comprehensive survey on PINNs covering theory, applications (fluid dynamics, solid mechanics, heat transfer, biomedical engineering), and future directions. Discusses theoretical aspects of PINN training including spectral bias, gradient pathology, and neural tangent kernel analysis. Reviews advanced architectures including domain decomposition (XPINNs), adaptive activation functions, and Fourier feature embeddings.
relevance: Strong theoretical foundation for understanding PINN training dynamics and convergence issues. Relevant for designing stable physics-informed training procedures for structural surrogate models.

title: Learning Mesh-Based Simulation with Graph Networks
venue: International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR), Spotlight
summary: Introduces MeshGraphNets, a GNN framework for learning physics simulations on meshes. Uses encode-process-decode architecture operating on mesh-adapted graphs with learned message passing. Demonstrates 1-2 orders of magnitude speedup over classical solvers on cloth, structural mechanics, aerodynamics, and fluid dynamics benchmarks. Handles arbitrary mesh topologies and can generalize to unseen meshes.
relevance: Directly relevant architectural reference: MeshGraphNets' encoder-processor-decoder paradigm on mesh graphs is the closest existing work to the micro message passing component in multi-scale PI-HGNN. The processor-style iterative message passing on element-node graphs informs the design of typed physical message passing for beam/plate elements.

title: Physics-Informed Graph Learning
venue: 22nd IEEE International Conference on Data Mining Workshops (ICDMW)
summary: Dedicated survey on physics-informed graph learning. Proposes a unified framework and examines PIGL methods through the lenses of incorporating physics rules (conservation laws, symmetries, dynamics) into graph learning. Discusses how physical constraints improve generalization, robustness, and interpretability of graph models.
relevance: Directly relevant as a survey of the intersection of physics-informed ML with graph learning. Provides the conceptual basis for physics-informed message passing and physics-constrained graph representations, which are central to the PI-HGNN framework.

title: Elastic structural analysis based on graph neural network without labeled data
summary: Proposes StructGNN-E, a physics-informed GNN framework for elastic analysis of bar structural systems without labeled data. Embeds equilibrium equations and constitutive relations as prior knowledge into the GNN loss function. Achieves ~99% prediction accuracy with 36% efficiency gain compared to FEM for large-scale frame structures. Demonstrates zero-shot transfer to irregular frame structures.
relevance: Highly relevant as one of the closest works to this thesis: GNN-based structural surrogate modeling with physics-informed loss (equilibrium + constitutive constraints). The approach of embedding bar system equilibrium into graph learning directly parallels the beam equilibrium loss planned for Stage 5 of this project.

title: On the use of mechanics-informed models to structural engineering systems: Application of graph neural networks for structural analysis
summary: Proposes a Mechanics-Informed Surrogate Model (MISM) using GNNs for structural analysis of 2D and 3D truss structures. Embeds the physics of the structural system explicitly into the surrogate model, moving beyond black-box ML. GNN naturally encodes the structural topology (nodes = joints, edges = members) and predicts deformations under various loading conditions.
relevance: Directly relevant as a GNN-based structural surrogate model that uses mechanics-informed design rather than pure data-driven learning. The truss/frame GNN approach informs how to leverage structural topology in the message passing architecture.

title: Physics-informed MeshGraphNets (PI-MGNs): Neural finite element solvers for non-stationary and nonlinear simulations on arbitrary meshes
venue: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering
summary: Combines PINNs with MeshGraphNets to create PI-MGNs that solve time-dependent nonlinear PDEs on arbitrary meshes without labeled simulation data. The physics-informed loss replaces the need for ground-truth training data by enforcing PDE residuals. Scales to large complex meshes and handles non-stationary problems. Demonstrates on thermal process simulations.
relevance: Directly relevant as an integration of PINN physics loss with MeshGraphNet-style GNN architecture. The approach of replacing supervised loss with physics-informed loss on mesh-based GNNs provides a template for adding physics-informed objectives to the PI-HGNN micro message passing stage.

title: Predicting dynamic responses of continuous deformable bodies: A graph-based learning approach
venue: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering
summary: Introduces PIERS (Physics-Informed Edge Recurrent Simulator), a GNN with recurrent modules for simulating elastic, plastic, and elastoplastic dynamics of continuous deformable bodies. The graph structure encodes mesh topology with physics-informed edge updates. Reduces prediction errors by 78-99% compared to baselines. Demonstrates strong generalization to unseen loading conditions and mesh resolutions.
relevance: Relevant as a physics-informed GNN for structural dynamics. The edge recurrent design with physics-informed updates and strong generalization to unseen conditions provides ideas for the dynamic/iterative message passing component and generalization evaluation of PI-HGNN.

title: Understanding Physics-Informed Neural Networks: Techniques, Applications, Trends, and Challenges
summary: Comprehensive PINN survey covering fundamental concepts, architectures, techniques for incorporating PDEs/ODEs, and challenges (computational complexity, data scarcity, spectral bias). Reviews over 200 papers. Discusses multi-objective optimization for balancing data and physics loss terms, and adaptive learning strategies.
relevance: Useful for understanding training challenges in physics-informed deep learning (e.g., gradient pathology, loss balancing) which are directly applicable when designing multi-task physics-informed objectives for structural surrogate models.

title: Physics-informed neural network for engineers: a review from an implementation aspect
venue: Journal of Mechanical Science and Technology
summary: Implementation-focused review of PINNs for engineering practitioners. Covers collocation point sampling strategies, optimization methods (L-BFGS, Adam), architecture improvements (adaptive activations, Fourier features, attention mechanisms), and practical considerations for engineering applications including hyperparameter tuning and loss weighting.
relevance: Practical implementation guidance for adding physics-informed losses to structural surrogate models. The engineering practitioner perspective directly addresses the challenges of deploying PINN-based methods in real structural analysis workflows.

title: Physics-informed Neural Networks (PINN) for computational solid mechanics: Numerical frameworks and applications
summary: Extensive review covering PINN frameworks, architectures, and applications in solid mechanics including constitutive modeling, damage/fracture mechanics, and inverse problems. Discusses opportunities and open challenges including multiscale modeling, finite deformation, and heterogeneous materials.
relevance: Directly relevant to structural engineering applications of PINNs. The discussion of constitutive modeling, damage/fracture, and solid mechanics provides context for extending structural surrogate models beyond linear elasticity.

title: A comprehensive review of advances in physics-informed neural networks and their applications in complex fluid dynamics
summary: Reviews PINN advances for turbulence, multiphase flows, multi-field coupled flows, and multiscale flows. Covers model architecture design, convergence optimization, and computational modules. Identifies key challenges in surrogate modeling for complex physics systems.
relevance: Although focused on fluid dynamics, the multiscale and multiphysics extension strategies are transferable to multiscale structural surrogate modeling. The discussion of convergence optimization for physics-informed training is broadly applicable.

title: Real-time full-field inference of displacement and stress from sparse local measurements using physics-informed neural networks
summary: PINN-based surrogate model that infers full-field displacement and stress from sparse strain measurements. Achieves millisecond-level real-time predictions without retraining for new measurement configurations. Tested on 2D plates with holes and center-cracked plates. Demonstrates digital twin capability for structural health monitoring.
relevance: Relevant as a PINN-based structural surrogate model for displacement and stress field inference. The real-time capability and sparse-to-full-field mapping are relevant for the dual decoder design (displacement + internal force prediction) in PI-HGNN.
